# Siro Framework — LLM Context
> This file helps AI assistants understand the Siro Framework architecture.
> Use this when helping developers write code with Siro.

## Overview
Siro is a zero-dependency PHP 8.2+ micro-framework for building APIs.
- 24,870 LOC, 0 external dependencies
- Namespace: `Siro\Core\`
- 1,312 tests passing, PHPStan level 7 (0 errors)
- Sub-millisecond boot, ~4MB RAM per request

## Key Classes
- `App` — Application bootstrap and request lifecycle
- `Router` — Route registration + middleware pipeline (onion model)
- `Model` — Active Record ORM with relationships, casts, scopes
- `Database` — PDO multi-connection manager (MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQLite)
- `Container` — DI container with autowiring
- `Request` — HTTP request with typed input helpers
- `Response` — JSON response builder with security headers
- `Validator` — Input validation engine (15+ rules)
- `Config` — Dot-notation config access
- `Cache` — File + Redis caching
- `Logger` — Structured logging with sanitization
- `Event` — Simple event emitter
- `Queue` — Database-backed job queue
- `Metrics` — Prometheus metrics collector

## Middleware
All implement `MiddlewareInterface::handle(Request, callable $next): mixed`
Available: auth, throttle, cors, json, audit, csp, version, etag, metrics

## API Versioning
Header-based: `Accept: application/vnd.siro.v2+json`
Access version: `$request->version()`

## Route Definition
```php
$router->get('/users', [UserController::class, 'index'])->middleware(['auth', 'throttle:60,1']);
$router->group('/api', ['auth'], function ($router) { ... });
```

## Response Helpers
```php
Response::success($data, 'message', 200);
Response::error('message', 400, ['field' => ['error']]);
Response::created($data, 'Created');
Response::noContent();
Response::paginated($data, $meta, 'list');
Response::raw('content', 'text/plain', 200);
```

## Model Features
- `$fillable`, `$hidden`, `$casts` (int, float, string, bool, array, datetime)
- Relationships: `hasMany()`, `hasOne()`, `belongsTo()`, `belongsToMany()`
- Scopes: `scopeActive()` → `Model::active()->get()`
- Eager loading: `Model::with('relation')->get()`
- Events: saving, saved, creating, created, updating, updated, deleting, deleted
- `find()`, `findOrFail()`, `firstOrCreate()`, `updateOrCreate()`
- `paginate($perPage, $page)` returns `{data: [...], meta: {page, per_page, total, last_page}}`

## Validation Rules
required, email, numeric, integer, min:N, max:N, confirmed, in:a,b, unique:table,column, exists:table,column, image, pdf, string, boolean, url, date, array, nullable

## Security
- JWT with HS256/RS256, refresh token rotation, token version revocation
- CSRF protection, rate limiting, CORS, CSP
- Input validation auto-returns 422
- Mass assignment protection via $fillable
- All SQL via PDO prepared statements
- Log sanitization for sensitive data

## CLI Commands
- `make:crud Name` — Scaffold full CRUD (model+controller+resource+migration+test)
- `make:auth` — Auth scaffolding
- `api:test GET /path` — Test endpoint from CLI
- `replay <trace_id>` — Replay production request
- `why` — Why last request failed
- `fix` — Watch + auto-replay on save
- `benchmark` — Performance benchmarking
- `migrate` — Run migrations
- `deploy` — One-command deployment
- `serve` — PHP dev server
- `config:cache` — Cache config for production

## Links
- Core: https://github.com/SiroSoft/siro-core
- Skeleton: https://github.com/SiroSoft/SiroPHP
- Docs: https://sirophp.com/docs
